assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions all slices will be affected but doesn't explicitly explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step rather than as a rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'All 4 slices (1024 TPU chips total) will be affected since multislice training requires synchronized execution'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Your training job will be completely interrupted' and 'The job won't gracefully pause - it will terminate when the maintenance window starts'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions in worst case section '8+ hours (if TPU resources become temporarily unavailable)' which addresses resource availability issues

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Plan to trigger a final checkpoint manually before the maintenance window'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Ensure your training code can resume from the latest checkpoint automatically' and shows job restart automation setup

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer the maintenance event

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows in terms of duration and timing but doesn't suggest using them to control when the event occurs or scheduling during planned breaks
